workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Just got this from Steven Wilson at Tailspin Toys -- I need help figuring out account health. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 16:58 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 14:29 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 17:01 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-A trap (Answers surface question, misses underlying ask); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-A failure mode (Answers surface question, misses underlying ask)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-A -- Answers surface question, misses underlying ask
